refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that conflates productivity measurement with operational capability. Refinement separates the measurement/verification aspect from the capability delivery aspect, and explicitly acknowledges dual operator modes (on-site and tele-operated) as distinct execution contexts. This clarification is essential because productivity depends on both machine capability and operator effectiveness, which differ between direct and remote control.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trials under defined conditions (soil type, task profile, operator experience level). Measure actual throughput (m³/h or ton/h) and compare against productivity_target. Log operator mode (on-site vs. remote), task completion time, and safety incidents. Verify compliance with worksite safety plan via audit checklist.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall provide sufficient power, speed, and digging/loading capacity to achieve the productivity target under representative soil and task conditions.

FR-NGHE-001.2: The Teleoperation system shall enable remote operators to control the Vehicle with latency, situational awareness, and responsiveness sufficient to achieve productivity target parity with on-site operation.

FR-NGHE-001.3: The Safety Services system shall enforce operational constraints (speed limits, geofencing, load limits, emergency stop) that do not degrade productivity below the target when applied correctly.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE Solution shall provide real-time telemetry and task feedback to enable operator decision-making and task optimization toward the productivity target.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate a high-power hydraulic excavator platform with optimized bucket geometry, engine tuning, and cycle-time reduction algorithms to maximize throughput per unit time.

DP-B: Combine a capable base vehicle with adaptive teleoperation (low-latency video, haptic feedback, predictive control) and real-time productivity analytics to enable both on-site and remote operators to achieve target throughput.

DP-C: Deploy a modular architecture where Vehicle provides raw capability, Teleoperation provides remote control fidelity, Safety Services enforces operational guardrails, and a central Tasking/Analytics module optimizes task sequencing and operator guidance to meet productivity targets.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Deploy a modular architecture where Vehicle provides raw capability, Teleoperation provides remote control fidelity, Safety Services enforces operational guardrails, and a central Tasking/Analytics module optimizes task sequencing and operator guidance to meet productivity targets.

potential_sources: Vehicle capability (power, speed, bucket capacity) must be sized to support target throughput; undersizing creates a hard floor on productivity regardless of operator skill or teleoperation quality.

Teleoperation latency and situational awareness (video quality, sensor fusion) directly impact remote operator effectiveness; poor teleoperation design will degrade remote productivity below on-site baseline.

Safety Services constraints (speed governors, load limits, geofencing) must be tuned to enforce safety without unnecessarily throttling productivity; overly conservative constraints couple safety to productivity loss.
Tasking/Analytics module must integrate telemetry from Vehicle and Teleoperation to provide real-time feedback and task optimization; weak integration will leave productivity gains on the table.
coupling_check.notes: DP-C is modular but exhibits controlled coupling: each module has a clear responsibility, but their combined behavior determines productivity outcome. The coupling is acceptable because it is explicit and can be managed via interface contracts (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link). No single module can achieve the FR in isolation; all four must work in concert.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: The Vehicle platform (base excavator or loader) has sufficient inherent power and speed to support the productivity_target; if the base machine is underpowered, no amount of teleoperation or analytics will compensate.
ASSUMPTION: Network infrastructure (latency, bandwidth, reliability) is adequate to support low-latency teleoperation; if network is poor, remote operator productivity will be severely degraded.
ASSUMPTION: Operators (on-site and remote) have adequate training and experience; untrained operators will not achieve target productivity regardless of system capability.
RISK: Safety Services constraints may be tuned too conservatively, creating artificial productivity ceiling. Requires careful calibration and validation.
RISK: Teleoperation latency may exceed operator tolerance threshold, making remote operation impractical for fine-control tasks (e.g., precision digging). Requires latency budget analysis.
RISK: Tasking/Analytics module may not provide sufficient real-time guidance; operators may revert to ad-hoc task execution, losing productivity gains.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-C to explicitly specify: (1) Vehicle capability targets (power, speed, bucket capacity, cycle time) derived from productivity_target and representative task profile; (2) Teleoperation latency budget (e.g., <200 ms round-trip) and situational awareness requirements (e.g., 360° video, obstacle detection); (3) Safety Services constraint tuning methodology (e.g., speed governor set to 80% of max safe speed, load limit set to 90% of structural limit); (4) Tasking/Analytics real-time feedback cadence (e.g., productivity KPI updated every 5 minutes, task recommendations every 10 minutes).

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level integration module that orchestrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and Tasking/Analytics to deliver the productivity capability. It owns the end-to-end productivity KPI measurement, operator mode selection (on-site vs. remote), and task execution workflow. It does not own the detailed implementation of any sub-module but is responsible for their integration and performance.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits real-time telemetry (Vehicle state, operator mode, task progress, productivity metrics, safety status) to external monitoring systems (Site Manager dashboard, Maintenance systems, Analytics platforms).

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives task definitions and operator commands from Site Manager or Tele-operator via Tasking interface (e.g., 'excavate zone A', 'load truck B', 'move to staging area').

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits safety alerts (geofence breach, load limit exceeded, emergency stop triggered, operator incapacity detected) to Safety Manager and on-site safety personnel.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution maintains network connectivity for teleoperation, telemetry streaming, and remote task dispatch. Network Link is bidirectional and critical for remote operator mode.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for the entire productivity capability. It owns the integration logic, operator mode selection, and KPI measurement. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) cross the boundary. This is the correct assignment.

Vehicle module owns the physical machine (engine, hydraulics, bucket, drivetrain) but does not own the teleoperation control logic, safety constraint enforcement, or productivity analytics. Assigning the entire FR to Vehicle would require Vehicle to own Teleoperation and Safety Services, creating high diffusion and poor separation of concerns.

Teleoperation module owns remote control fidelity and operator interface but does not own the Vehicle capability, safety constraint enforcement, or productivity analytics. Assigning the entire FR to Teleoperation would require it to own Vehicle and Safety Services, creating high diffusion.

Safety Services module owns constraint enforcement (speed governors, load limits, geofencing) but does not own the Vehicle capability, teleoperation control, or productivity analytics. Assigning the entire FR to Safety Services would require it to own Vehicle and Teleoperation, creating high diffusion and conflating safety with productivity.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the correct owner of FR-NGHE-001 because: (1) Productivity is an emergent property of the integrated system, not a property of any single sub-module; (2) NGHE Solution owns the integration logic that coordinates Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and Tasking/Analytics; (3) NGHE Solution owns the KPI measurement and reporting (Telemetry interface); (4) NGHE Solution owns the operator mode selection and task execution workflow; (5) All external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are natural outputs/inputs of the integrated system, not of individual sub-modules.
containment_risks: RISK: NGHE Solution may become a 'god module' that owns too much integration logic, making it difficult to test and maintain. Mitigation: Define clear responsibilities for each sub-module (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, Tasking/Analytics) and use well-defined interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) to decouple them.

RISK: Productivity measurement may be ambiguous or disputed (e.g., how to account for idle time, task switching, operator breaks). Mitigation: Define a clear KPI calculation methodology in the Telemetry interface specification and validate it with stakeholders before implementation.
RISK: Operator mode selection (on-site vs. remote) may create mode-switching latency or inconsistency. Mitigation: Define clear mode-switching logic and test it under representative conditions.

RISK: Network Link may be unavailable or degraded, preventing remote operation. Mitigation: Define fallback behavior (e.g., revert to on-site operation, activate emergency stop) and test it under poor network conditions.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Refine the NGHE Solution IM boundary to explicitly define: (1) Integration responsibilities (operator mode selection, task execution workflow, KPI measurement); (2) Sub-module responsibilities (Vehicle: raw capability; Teleoperation: remote control fidelity; Safety Services: constraint enforcement; Tasking/Analytics: task optimization); (3) Interface contracts (Telemetry: KPI calculation, update cadence; Tasking: task format, command semantics; SafetyAlert: alert types, severity levels; Network Link: latency budget, bandwidth requirements); (4) Fallback and degradation modes (e.g., network loss, operator incapacity, safety constraint activation).

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (modular architecture with Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and Tasking/Analytics) is a sound solution to FR-NGHE-001 (achieve productivity target). The DP explicitly addresses the key drivers of productivity: (1) Vehicle capability (power, speed, bucket capacity); (2) Teleoperation fidelity (latency, situational awareness); (3) Safety constraint tuning (no unnecessary throttling); (4) Tasking/Analytics optimization (real-time feedback, task guidance). The DP is modular and allows independent optimization of each sub-system while maintaining integration. No fundamental mismatch between FR and DP.

diffusion_risks: Productivity measurement logic may be diffused across multiple modules (Vehicle telemetry, Teleoperation operator feedback, Safety Services constraint status, Tasking/Analytics KPI calculation). Mitigation: Centralize KPI calculation in NGHE Solution and define a clear Telemetry interface contract.

Operator mode selection logic may be diffused across Teleoperation (mode switching) and NGHE Solution (mode coordination). Mitigation: Define clear ownership in NGHE Solution and use Tasking interface to communicate mode changes.

Safety constraint tuning may require coordination between Safety Services (constraint enforcement) and Vehicle (capability limits). Mitigation: Define constraint tuning methodology in Safety Services and validate against Vehicle capability via integration testing.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct container for the entire FR because it owns the integration logic and KPI measurement. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are sub-modules that contribute to the overall productivity capability but do not own the FR individually. Tasking/Analytics is a supporting module that provides real-time optimization. All external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are natural outputs/inputs of the integrated NGHE Solution.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of FR-NGHE-001 is feasible via representative operations trials: (1) Conduct trials under defined conditions (soil type, task profile, operator experience); (2) Measure actual throughput (m³/h or ton/h) and compare against productivity_target; (3) Log operator mode, task completion time, and safety incidents; (4) Verify compliance with worksite safety plan via audit checklist. Verification requires access to representative worksite and operators, which is a reasonable assumption for acceptance testing.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of FR-NGHE-001 to NGHE Solution is clear and unambiguous. NGHE Solution owns the end-to-end productivity capability and is responsible for integrating Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and Tasking/Analytics to achieve the target. Sub-module responsibilities are well-defined and can be allocated to lower-level FRs in the next decomposition level.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-C to specify Vehicle capability targets (power, speed, bucket capacity, cycle time) derived from productivity_target and representative task profile. This will enable Vehicle sub-module to be designed and verified independently.

Refine DP-C to specify Teleoperation latency budget (<200 ms round-trip) and situational awareness requirements (360° video, obstacle detection). This will enable Teleoperation sub-module to be designed and verified independently.

Refine DP-C to specify Safety Services constraint tuning methodology (e.g., speed governor set to 80% of max safe speed). This will enable Safety Services sub-module to be designed and verified independently.

Refine DP-C to specify Tasking/Analytics real-time feedback cadence (e.g., productivity KPI updated every 5 minutes). This will enable Tasking/Analytics sub-module to be designed and verified independently.

Define a clear KPI calculation methodology in the Telemetry interface specification (e.g., how to account for idle time, task switching, operator breaks) and validate it with stakeholders before implementation.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that requires decomposition into sub-FRs to enable detailed design and verification. The refined FR has identified four sub-FRs (Vehicle capability, Teleoperation fidelity, Safety Services constraint tuning, Tasking/Analytics optimization) that are each atomic and can be assigned to sub-modules. DP-C (modular architecture) is sound and passes the FR-DP gate. NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary and passes the DP-IM gate. However, the FR is not yet atomic enough for detailed design; further decomposition is required to specify Vehicle capability targets, Teleoperation latency budgets, Safety Services constraint tuning, and Tasking/Analytics feedback cadence. Recommend proceeding to the next decomposition level to refine each sub-FR and assign it to the corresponding sub-module (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, Tasking/Analytics).
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall provide sufficient power, speed, and digging/loading capacity to achieve the productivity target under representative soil and task conditions.

FR-NGHE-001.2: The Teleoperation system shall enable remote operators to control the Vehicle with latency, situational awareness, and responsiveness sufficient to achieve productivity target parity with on-site operation.

FR-NGHE-001.3: The Safety Services system shall enforce operational constraints (speed limits, geofencing, load limits, emergency stop) that do not degrade productivity below the target when applied correctly.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE Solution shall provide real-time telemetry and task feedback to enable operator decision-making and task optimization toward the productivity target.

assumptions: The Vehicle platform (base excavator or loader) has sufficient inherent power and speed to support the productivity_target.
Network infrastructure (latency, bandwidth, reliability) is adequate to support low-latency teleoperation.
Operators (on-site and remote) have adequate training and experience.
Worksite conditions (soil type, task profile, weather) are representative of the agreed evaluation period.
Safety plan and operational policies are well-defined and can be enforced via Safety Services constraints.
Productivity measurement methodology (m³/h or ton/h) is unambiguous and can be calculated from telemetry data.
open_risks: Safety Services constraints may be tuned too conservatively, creating artificial productivity ceiling.
Teleoperation latency may exceed operator tolerance threshold, making remote operation impractical.
Tasking/Analytics module may not provide sufficient real-time guidance, leaving productivity gains on the table.
Network Link may be unavailable or degraded, preventing remote operation.
Operator mode switching may create latency or inconsistency.
Productivity measurement may be ambiguous or disputed (e.g., how to account for idle time, task switching, operator breaks).
evidence_needed: Vehicle capability specification (power, speed, bucket capacity, cycle time) derived from productivity_target and representative task profile.
Teleoperation latency budget and situational awareness requirements specification.
Safety Services constraint tuning methodology and validation against Vehicle capability.
Tasking/Analytics real-time feedback cadence and optimization algorithm specification.
Network Link latency and bandwidth requirements specification.
KPI calculation methodology specification and stakeholder validation.
Representative operations trial plan and acceptance criteria.
Operator training and experience requirements specification.
